tools.name: browser_close
tools.description: Close the page

tools.name: browser_resize
tools.description: Resize the browser window

tools.name: browser_console_messages
tools.description: Returns all console messages

level.description: Level of the console messages to return. Each level includes the messages of more severe levels. Defaults to "info".

all.description: Return all console messages since the beginning of the session, not just since the last navigation. Defaults to false.

filename.description: Filename to save the console messages to. If not provided, messages are returned as text.

tools.name: browser_handle_dialog
tools.description: Handle a dialog

promptText.description: The text of the prompt in case of a prompt dialog.

tools.name: browser_evaluate
tools.description: Evaluate JavaScript expression on page or element

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

function.description: () => { /* code */ } or (element) => { /* code */ } when element is provided

filename.description: Filename to save the result to. If not provided, result is returned as text.

tools.name: browser_file_upload
tools.description: Upload one or multiple files

paths.description: The absolute paths to the files to upload. Can be single file or multiple files. If omitted, file chooser is cancelled.

tools.name: browser_drop
tools.description: Drop files or MIME-typed data onto an element, as if dragged from outside the page. At least one of "paths" or "data" must be provided.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

paths.description: Absolute paths to files to drop onto the element.

data.description: Data to drop, as a map of MIME type to string value (e.g. {"text/plain": "hello", "text/uri-list": "https://example.com"}).

tools.name: browser_fill_form
tools.description: Fill multiple form fields

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

value.description: Value to fill in the field. If the field is a checkbox, the value should be `true` or `false`. If the field is a combobox, the value should be the text of the option.

tools.name: browser_press_key
tools.description: Press a key on the keyboard

key.description: Name of the key to press or a character to generate, such as `ArrowLeft` or `a`

tools.name: browser_type
tools.description: Type text into editable element

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

text.description: Text to type into the element

submit.description: Whether to submit entered text (press Enter after)

slowly.description: Whether to type one character at a time. Useful for triggering key handlers in the page. By default entire text is filled in at once.

tools.name: browser_navigate
tools.description: Navigate to a URL

tools.name: browser_navigate_back
tools.description: Go back to the previous page in the history

tools.name: browser_network_requests
tools.description: Returns a numbered list of network requests since loading the page. Use browser_network_request with the number to get full details.

static.description: Whether to include successful static resources like images, fonts, scripts, etc. Defaults to false.

filter.description: Only return requests whose URL matches this regexp (e.g. "/api/.*user").

filename.description: Filename to save the network requests to. If not provided, requests are returned as text.

tools.name: browser_network_request
tools.description: Returns full details (headers and body) of a single network request, or a single part if `part` is set. Use the number from browser_network_requests.

index.description: 1-based index of the request, as printed by browser_network_requests.

part.description: Return only this part of the request. Omit to return full details.

filename.description: Filename to save the result to. If not provided, output is returned as text.

tools.name: browser_run_code_unsafe
tools.description: Run a Playwright code snippet. Unsafe: executes arbitrary JavaScript in the Playwright server process and is RCE-equivalent.

code.description: A JavaScript function containing Playwright code to execute. It will be invoked with a single argument, page, which you can use for any page interaction. For example: `async (page) => { await page.getByRole('button', { name: 'Submit' }).click(); return await page.title(); }`

filename.description: Load code from the specified file. If both code and filename are provided, code will be ignored.

tools.name: browser_take_screenshot
tools.description: Take a screenshot of the current page. You can't perform actions based on the screenshot, use browser_snapshot for actions.

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

type.description: Image format for the screenshot. Default is png.

filename.description: File name to save the screenshot to. Defaults to `page-{timestamp}.{png|jpeg}` if not specified. Prefer relative file names to stay within the output directory.

fullPage.description: When true, takes a screenshot of the full scrollable page, instead of the currently visible viewport. Cannot be used with element screenshots.

tools.name: browser_snapshot
tools.description: Capture accessibility snapshot of the current page, this is better than screenshot

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

filename.description: Save snapshot to markdown file instead of returning it in the response.

depth.description: Limit the depth of the snapshot tree

boxes.description: Include each element's bounding box as [box=x,y,width,height] in the snapshot. Coordinates are viewport-relative, in CSS pixels (Element.getBoundingClientRect)

tools.name: browser_click
tools.description: Perform click on a web page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

doubleClick.description: Whether to perform a double click instead of a single click

button.description: Button to click, defaults to left

tools.name: browser_drag
tools.description: Perform drag and drop between two elements

startElement.description: Human-readable source element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

startTarget.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

endElement.description: Human-readable target element description used to obtain the permission to interact with the element

endTarget.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

tools.name: browser_hover
tools.description: Hover over element on page

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

tools.name: browser_select_option
tools.description: Select an option in a dropdown

element.description: Human-readable element description used to obtain permission to interact with the element

target.description: Exact target element reference from the page snapshot, or a unique element selector

values.description: Array of values to select in the dropdown. This can be a single value or multiple values.

tools.name: browser_tabs
tools.description: List, create, close, or select a browser tab.

index.description: Tab index, used for close/select. If omitted for close, current tab is closed.

url.description: URL to navigate to in the new tab, used for new.

tools.name: browser_wait_for
tools.description: Wait for text to appear or disappear or a specified time to pass

time.description: The time to wait in seconds

textGone.description: The text to wait for to disappear

tools.name: steampipe_query
tools.description:
Query cloud infrastructure, SaaS, APIs, code and more with SQL.
  
  Queries are read-only and must use PostgreSQL syntax. 

  For best performance: limit columns requested, use materialized CTEs instead
  of joins. Trust the search path unless sure you need to specify a schema.

  Check available tables and columns before querying using steampipe_table_list
  and steampipe_table_show.

sql.description: The SQL query to execute. Must use PostgreSQL syntax and be read-only.

tools.name: steampipe_table_list
tools.description: List all available Steampipe tables. Use schema and filter parameters to narrow down results.

schema.description: Optional schema name to filter tables by. If not provided, lists tables from all schemas.

filter.description: Optional filter pattern to match against table names. Use ILIKE syntax, including % as a wildcard.

tools.name: steampipe_table_show
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific Steampipe table, including column definitions, data types, and descriptions.

name.description: The name of the table to show details for. Can be schema qualified (e.g. 'aws_account' or 'aws.aws_account').

schema.description: Optional schema name. If provided, only searches in this schema. If not provided, searches across all schemas.

tools.name: steampipe_plugin_list
tools.description: List all Steampipe plugins installed on the system. Plugins provide access to different data sources like AWS, GCP, or Azure.

tools.name: steampipe_plugin_show
tools.description: Get details for a specific Steampipe plugin installation, including version, memory limits, and configuration.

name.description: Name of the plugin to show details for

resources.name: status
resources.description: Get the current status of the Steampipe connection

tools.name: resolve-library-id
tools.description:
Resolves a package/product name to a Context7-compatible library ID and returns matching libraries.

You MUST call this function before 'Query Documentation' tool to obtain a valid Context7-compatible library ID UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Each result includes:
- Library ID: Context7-compatible identifier (format: /org/project)
- Name: Library or package name
- Description: Short summary
- Code Snippets: Number of available code examples
- Source Reputation: Authority indicator (High, Medium, Low, or Unknown)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)
- Versions: List of versions if available. Use one of those versions if the user provides a version in their query. The format of the version is /org/project/version.

For best results, select libraries based on name match, source reputation, snippet coverage, benchmark score, and relevance to your use case.

Selection Process:
1. Analyze the query to understand what library/package the user is looking for
2. Return the most relevant match based on:
- Name similarity to the query (exact matches prioritized)
- Description relevance to the query's intent
- Documentation coverage (prioritize libraries with higher Code Snippet counts)
- Source reputation (consider libraries with High or Medium reputation more authoritative)
- Benchmark Score: Quality indicator (100 is the highest score)

Response Format:
- Return the selected library ID in a clearly marked section
- Provide a brief explanation for why this library was chosen
- If multiple good matches exist, acknowledge this but proceed with the most relevant one
- If no good matches exist, clearly state this and suggest query refinements

For ambiguous queries, request clarification before proceeding with a best-guess match.

IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question. If you cannot find what you need after 3 calls, use the best result you have.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. This is used to rank library results by relevance to what the user is trying to accomplish. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

libraryName.description: Library name to search for and retrieve a Context7-compatible library ID. Use the official library name with proper punctuation — e.g., 'Next.js' instead of 'nextjs', 'Customer.io' instead of 'customerio', 'Three.js' instead of 'threejs'.

tools.name: query-docs
tools.description:
Retrieves and queries up-to-date documentation and code examples from Context7 for any programming library or framework.

You must call 'Resolve Context7 Library ID' tool first to obtain the exact Context7-compatible library ID required to use this tool, UNLESS the user explicitly provides a library ID in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version' in their query.

Do not call this tool more than 3 times per question.

libraryId.description: Exact Context7-compatible library ID (e.g., '/mongodb/docs', '/vercel/next.js', '/supabase/supabase', '/vercel/next.js/v14.3.0-canary.87') retrieved from 'resolve-library-id' or directly from user query in the format '/org/project' or '/org/project/version'.

query.description: The question or task you need help with. Be specific and include relevant details. Good: 'How to set up authentication with JWT in Express.js' or 'React useEffect cleanup function examples'. Bad: 'auth' or 'hooks'. The query is sent to the Context7 API for processing. Do not include any sensitive or confidential information such as API keys, passwords, credentials, personal data, or proprietary code in your query.

tools.name: get-selected-cell
tools.description: This tool retrieves the selected cell on the target page of the current Draw.io document. If the target page is not currently visible, Draw.io may switch the visible page first because selection is UI-bound.

tools.name: add-rectangle
tools.description: This tool allows you to add a new Rectangle vertex cell on the target page of the current Draw.io document.

tools.name: add-edge
tools.description: This tool creates an edge, sometimes called also a relation, between two vertexes (cells). When source and target are the same shape (self-connector), a loop edge style is automatically applied if no custom style is provided.

tools.name: delete-cell-by-id
tools.description: Deletes a cell from the target page, whether it is a vertex or edge.

tools.name: get-shape-categories
tools.description: Retrieves available shape categories from the diagram's library. Library is split into multiple categories.

tools.name: get-shapes-in-category
tools.description: Retrieve all shapes in the provided category from the diagram's library. A shape primarily contains `style` based on which you can create new vertex cells.

tools.name: get-shape-by-name
tools.description: Retrieve a specific shape by its name from all available shapes in the diagram's library. It returns the shape and also the category it belongs.

tools.name: add-cell-of-shape
tools.description: This tool allows you to add a new vertex cell on the target page of the current Draw.io document by its shape name.

tools.name: set-cell-shape
tools.description: Updates the visual style of an existing vertex cell to match a library shape by name.

tools.name: set-cell-data
tools.description: Sets or updates a custom attribute on an existing cell.

tools.name: edit-cell
tools.description: Update properties of an existing vertex/shape cell by its ID. Only provided fields are modified; unspecified properties remain unchanged.

tools.name: edit-edge
tools.description: Update properties of an existing edge by its ID. Only provided fields are modified; unspecified properties remain unchanged. Supports setting waypoints for edge geometry control.

tools.name: list-paged-model
tools.description: Retrieves a paginated view of all cells (vertices and edges) on the target page. This tool provides access to the complete model data with essential fields only, sanitized to remove circular dependencies and excessive data.

tools.name: list-layers
tools.description: Lists all available layers on the target page with their IDs and names.

tools.name: set-active-layer
tools.description: Sets the active layer for creating new elements. All subsequent element creation will happen in this layer. If the target page is not currently visible, Draw.io may switch the visible page first because active-layer state is UI-bound.

tools.name: move-cell-to-layer
tools.description: Moves a cell from its current layer to a target layer.

tools.name: set-cell-parent
tools.description: Sets the parent of a cell, making it a child of the specified parent cell. This allows creating hierarchical relationships where moving the parent also moves its children.

tools.name: get-active-layer
tools.description: Gets the currently active layer information for the target page. If the target page is not currently visible, Draw.io may switch the visible page first because active-layer state is UI-bound.

tools.name: create-layer
tools.description: Creates a new layer on the target page.

tools.name: export-diagram
tools.description: Export the target page or current diagram as SVG, PNG, or XML. Returns the diagram data as base64 (PNG) or text (SVG/XML). Optionally saves to a file.

tools.name: import-diagram
tools.description: Import a diagram from XML, SVG with embedded XML, or PNG with embedded XML into the current Draw.io instance. `target_page` is required for `replace` and `add`, and optional for `new-page`.

tools.name: import-mermaid
tools.description: Insert a Mermaid diagram into the target Draw.io page. Conversion runs inside the Draw.io editor via its bundled Mermaid pipeline (EditorUi.parseMermaidDiagram). mode='native' converts supported Mermaid types into native mxGraph cells; unsupported types fall back to an embedded image cell. mode='embed' always emits a single image cell that preserves the Mermaid source for re-editing inside Draw.io. `target_page` is required for `replace` and `add`, and optional for `new-page`.

tools.name: list-documents
tools.description: Lists all connected Draw.io document instances with their stable instance IDs and basic metadata.

tools.name: list-pages
tools.description: Lists all pages in the current Draw.io document with their index, id, name, and current-page flag.

tools.name: get-current-page
tools.description: Gets the currently visible page metadata, including index, id, name, and whether it is active.

tools.name: create-page
tools.description: Appends a new blank page to the current Draw.io document without changing the visible page when the runtime supports background page insertion.

tools.name: copy-page
tools.description: Creates a copy of an existing page in the target/current Draw.io document, appends the copy to the end of the page list, and returns the copied page metadata. When possible, the previously visible page is restored after the copy is created.

tools.name: rename-page
tools.description: Renames the selected page without changing the visible page.
